description:
A cloud-architect colleague has handed you a small,
  tab-separated inventory that lists every internal service currently deployed.
  You must prepare two artefacts — a comma-separated list of all services that
  need to be migrated and a short plaintext summary — using nothing but awk and
  sed (no other text utilities such as cut, grep, tr …).

  1. Source file  
     • Location   : /home/user/migration_data/services.tsv  
     • Format     : TAB-separated (first row is a header).  
       Columns (in order)  
         1. service_id  
         2. service_name  
         3. environment  
         4. owner  
         5. port  
         6. status  

  2. Target file #1 — the CSV list  
     • You must create /home/user/migration_data/services_to_migrate.csv  
     • Include ONLY the rows whose “status” column is literally migrate  
     • Discard the header row  
     • Keep the following four columns and in that order  
         1. service_id  
         2. service_name  
         3. owner  
         4. port  
     • Use a literal comma (,) as field separator, no spaces  
     • The file must end with a single trailing newline (<LF>).  
     • Expected final contents (exactly three lines):  
         1005,orders-api,team-alpha,443  
         1006,shipping-api,team-beta,443  
         1007,analytics-batch,team-delta,9000  

  3. Target file #2 — the human summary  
     • Create /home/user/migration_data/migration_summary.log  
     • Plain ASCII text, four lines, each terminated with <LF>.  
     • Exact text to produce:  
         Total services scanned: 8  
         Services marked for migration: 3  
         CSV file generated at: /home/user/migration_data/services_to_migrate.csv  
         Operation completed successfully.  

  4. Constraints  
     • Solve the whole task with a simple sequence of 3–4 shell commands that
       use only awk and/or sed.  
     • Do NOT edit the source TSV manually; derive every result from it.  
     • Make sure both output files are created in the directory
       /home/user/migration_data with default user-level permissions (664 for
       files is fine).  

  When you are done, you can open the two result files to visually verify their
  contents. Our automated tests will 1) checksum both generated files and 2)
  ensure you used only awk and sed in the command history.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file that the container will already hold -->
  <source-file path="/home/user/migration_data/services.tsv"><![CDATA[
service_id	service_name	environment	owner	port	status
1001	auth-api	prod	team-alpha	443	active
1002	payment-api	stage	team-beta	8080	active
1003	legacy-mq	prod	team-legacy	61616	deprecated
1004	catalog-web	dev	team-gamma	80	active
1005	orders-api	prod	team-alpha	443	migrate
1006	shipping-api	prod	team-beta	443	migrate
1007	analytics-batch	stage	team-delta	9000	migrate
1008	billing-web	prod	team-gamma	443	active
]]></source-file>

  <!-- Expected artefact #1 -->
  <expected-file path="/home/user/migration_data/services_to_migrate.csv"><![CDATA[
1005,orders-api,team-alpha,443
1006,shipping-api,team-beta,443
1007,analytics-batch,team-delta,9000
]]></expected-file>

  <!-- Expected artefact #2 -->
  <expected-file path="/home/user/migration_data/migration_summary.log"><![CDATA[
Total services scanned: 8
Services marked for migration: 3
CSV file generated at: /home/user/migration_data/services_to_migrate.csv
Operation completed successfully.
]]></expected-file>
name: task_000000_a2ad4998
